title: Lessingianthus parvifolius
taxonomy: Lessingianthus parvifolius is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the flowering plants, specifically within the order Asterales. Lessingianthus parvifolius is native to South America, which is consistent with the geographical distribution of the Asteraceae family. Its classification suggests a close relationship with other daisy-like plants in the same family. Further research may be needed to determine its exact classification within the Asteraceae family.

intro: Lessingianthus parvifolius, also known as Parvifolius, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which may offer relief from congestion and promote healthy digestion. The bioactive constituents of Lessingianthus parvifolius include flavonoids, saponins, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often used to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to boost the immune system.
